title: ... Standing to one side, that devata spoke this verse in the presence of the Blessed One: For one fond of conceit, Here there is…
content:
... Standing to one side, that devata spoke this verse in the presence of the Blessed One:
For one fond of conceit,
Here there is no taming,
No radiant wisdom,
No concentrated mind,
Living alone in the forest,
With a heedless mind,
He cannot cross beyond
Māra's power.
( Blessed One ):
Abandoning all conceit,
Mind well concentrated,
With mind well examined,
Liberated from all stains,
Living alone in the forest,
With mind not heedless,
That one crosses beyond
Māra's power.

title: In whatever families, bhikkhus, the parents are honored and revered by the children in the home, those families are considered like Brahmā. In whatever families,…
content: In whatever families, bhikkhus, the parents are honored and revered by the children in the home, those families are considered like Brahmā. In whatever families, bhikkhus, the parents are honored and revered by the children in the home, those families are considered like the ancient teachers. In whatever families, bhikkhus, the parents are honored and revered by the children in the home, those families, bhikkhus, are considered like the devas of old. In whatever families, bhikkhus, the parents are honored and revered by the children in the home, those families are considered worthy of offerings.

title: Good friends, furthermore, here someone has coarse bodily conduct not yet calmed, coarse verbal conduct not yet calmed, coarse mental conduct not yet calmed. After…
content: Good friends, furthermore, here someone has coarse bodily conduct not yet calmed, coarse verbal conduct not yet calmed, coarse mental conduct not yet calmed. After some time, he hears the noble Dhamma, gives proper attention, and attains the true Dhamma and the Dhamma in conformity with it. Having heard the noble Dhamma, given proper attention, and attained the true Dhamma and the Dhamma in conformity with it, his coarse bodily conduct becomes calmed, his coarse verbal conduct becomes calmed, his coarse mental conduct becomes calmed. His bodily conduct being calmed, his verbal conduct being calmed, his mental conduct being calmed, ease arises, and more than ease, joy arises. Good friends, just as from comfort joy is born, so too, good friends, because his coarse bodily conduct is calmed, his coarse verbal conduct is calmed, his coarse mental conduct is calmed, ease arises, and more than ease, joy arises. Good friends, this is the second short path to well-being, realized by the Blessed One, the Knower, the Seer, the Worthy One, the Perfectly Enlightened One.

title: Bhikkhus, these four cases are to be understood with four cases. What four? Bhikkhus, by living together, virtue is to be understood — thus, over…
content: Bhikkhus, these four cases are to be understood with four cases. What four? Bhikkhus, by living together, virtue is to be understood — thus, over a long time, not otherwise, with attention, not without attention, with wisdom, not with poor wisdom. Bhikkhus, by doing a task together, purity of conduct is to be understood — thus, over a long time, not otherwise, with attention, not without attention, with wisdom, not with poor wisdom. Bhikkhus, in times of misfortune, fortitude is to be understood — thus, over a long time, not otherwise, with attention, not without attention, with wisdom, not with poor wisdom. Bhikkhus, by discussion, wisdom is to be understood — thus, over a long time, not otherwise, with attention, not without attention, with wisdom, not with poor wisdom.

title: - Bhikkhus, what matter are you now discussing? What conversation has been interrupted among you? - Here, Venerable Sir, after the meal, returning from the…
content:
- Bhikkhus, what matter are you now discussing? What conversation has been interrupted among you?
- Here, Venerable Sir, after the meal, returning from the alms round, we gathered in the assembly hall, competing, disputing, quarrelling, wounding one another with verbal weapons.
- Bhikkhus, it is not proper for you, clansmen who have gone forth out of faith from home to homelessness, to live competing, disputing, quarrelling, wounding one another with verbal weapons. Bhikkhus, there are these ten things that are to be remembered, that create love, create respect, lead to concord, lead to non-dispute, to unity, to harmony. What ten?

title: Furthermore, venerable sirs, a bhikkhu is virtuous, living restrained by the restraint of the Pātimokkha, perfect in conduct and resort, seeing danger in the slightest…
content: Furthermore, venerable sirs, a bhikkhu is virtuous, living restrained by the restraint of the Pātimokkha, perfect in conduct and resort, seeing danger in the slightest faults, undertaking and training in the training rules. Venerable sirs, this is the second support for developing the states pertaining to enlightenment.

title: The door to the Deathless is open, For those who are willing to hear. Renounce your inaccurate faith. Thinking of the trouble, I did not…
content:
The door to the Deathless is open,
For those who are willing to hear.
Renounce your inaccurate faith.
Thinking of the trouble,
I did not want to teach
The supreme sublime Dhamma
Among humans.
(O Brahmā)

title: - This saṃsāra is without discoverable beginning, bhikkhus. A first point is not discerned of beings roaming and wandering on, shrouded by ignorance and fettered…
content: - This saṃsāra is without discoverable beginning, bhikkhus. A first point is not discerned of beings roaming and wandering on, shrouded by ignorance and fettered by craving. Bhikkhus, when you see a situation of misery and misfortune, you should conclude: 'We have experienced such for a long time.' Why is that? This saṃsāra is without discoverable beginning, bhikkhus ... enough to be liberated from all formations.

title: Just as, bhikkhus, in the autumn, when the sky is clear and cloudless, the sun rises in the sky, dispelling all darkness in space, and…
content: Just as, bhikkhus, in the autumn, when the sky is clear and cloudless, the sun rises in the sky, dispelling all darkness in space, and shines, blazes, and radiates; so too, for the noble disciple, the dust-free, stainless Dhamma-eye arises. And with this arising of knowledge and vision, bhikkhus, three fetters are abandoned: personality view, doubt, and clinging to rites and rituals. Moreover, he is freed from two things: greed and hatred. Secluded from sensual pleasures, secluded from unwholesome states, he enters and dwells in the first jhāna, which is accompanied by initial and sustained thought, with rapture and happiness born of seclusion. If, bhikkhus, at that time, that noble disciple should die, there is no fetter bound by which he might return to this world.

title: Defiled by hatred, brahmin, overcome, mind-possessed, one thinks of harming oneself, thinks of harming others, thinks of harming both, and experiences mental suffering and distress.…
content: Defiled by hatred, brahmin, overcome, mind-possessed, one thinks of harming oneself, thinks of harming others, thinks of harming both, and experiences mental suffering and distress. When hatred is abandoned, one does not think of harming oneself, does not think of harming others, does not think of harming both, and does not experience mental suffering and distress. Thus, brahmin, the Dhamma is directly visible, timeless, inviting investigation, onward-leading, to be personally experienced by the wise.
